title: narcissus × interjectus
intro: Narcissus × interjectus, also known as the Intermediate Daffodil, is a hybrid plant derived from the intersection of N. pseudonarcissus and N. pseudonarcissus var. pseudonarcissus, exhibiting characteristics from both parent species. The plant contains galantamine, a compound with medicinal properties that can be used to treat conditions such as Alzheimer's disease and other cognitive impairments. The health benefits of Narcissus × interjectus include its potential to improve memory and cognitive function, as well as its antioxidant properties, which can protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of the plant's flowers, leaves, or bulbs can be used in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and extracts, although it's essential to use them under the guidance of a medical professional due to potential toxicity and interactions.
